mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when unemployment remains above 7.0% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.03 when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 230.0 signal accelerating inflation that erodes real discount rates and triggers central bank tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 230.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of observations, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes when Fed forward guidance remains dovish.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month payroll gains and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates above 2.0 percent reflect sustained aggregate demand expansion that directly lifts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month indicates supply-side overheating that compresses profit margins and triggers inventory drawdowns, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 1.0% MoM is followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization remains below 80%.
